rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Receiver Kevin Singers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Instructions Re Receivers Use Of Sale Proceeds From Church And Guerrero Properties
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2021 line 1. OTHER KEVIN (RECEIVER) SINGER Receiver Kevin Singers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Instructions Re Receivers Use Of Sale Proceeds From Church And Guerrero Properties The $1,545,565.15 held by the receiver shall be distributed as follows: (1) payment on the outstanding judgment in the amount of $106,000 (Ex. ( to Singer Declaration); (2) payment for the soft story retrofit, $505,000 to be kept by the receiver and paid towards the retrofit; (3) $45,000 to be kept by the receiver and used for ongoing expenses, including costs of receivership; (4) the balance, $889,565.15, to be deposited with the Court. Any claims as to the use and distribution of the deposited amounts may be made by separate motions, including but not limited to the parties, third-party lenders and judgment creditors, receiver, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RNO FINANCIAL , LLC, VS. TIMOTHY R. GUZZETTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Interlocutory Order For Partition And Sale Of The Subject Property And Appointment Of Receiver

PLAINTIFF RNO FINANCIAL , LLC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Interlocutory Order For Partition And Sale Of The Subject Property And Appointment Of Receiver is OFF CALENDAR. rulings.Case Title: BUSH STREET APARTMENT GROUP, LLC. VS. BALMORAL RESIDENCES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Defaults Of Yale Residences And Tony Brettkelly
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT YALE RESIDENCES, LLC, TONY BRETTKELLY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Defaults Of Yale Residences And Tony Brettkelly is GRANTED. Defaults are set aside. No valid proof of service for summons and complaint on file. Cases cited in the supplemental opposition do not support a finding that service by mail of the summons and complaint on California residents was proper. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ZACHARY SAMUEL BERNSTEIN ET AL VS. J.P. MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in good faith (1) that instructions per CCP 2943.1(d) to close escrow were provided to the moving defendant; who provided those instructions and who was responsible for providing those instructions before close of escrow/disbursement of funds to the seller; and/or (2) that the moving defendant had actual notice of the title transfer of the property after sale was finalized, escrow closed and title was recorded. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 2490 CHESTNUT STREET, LLC VS. SOFIA PALUMBO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT SOFIA PALUMBO DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint is not uncertain, and defendant fails to sustain its burden to show that the complaint does not properly state a cause of action for unlawful detainer under the state law. While defendant addresses SFRO 37.9(n) amended on March 12, 2021, CCP 1179.05 is not addressed, ("(1) Any extension, expansion, renewal, reenactment, or new adoption of a measure, however delineated, that occurs between August 19, 2020, and June 30, 2021, shall have no effect before July 1, 2021.")=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
